summary.intent: Add a Slack slash command webhook endpoint that receives /command payloads via HTTP POST, mirroring the existing Socket Mode handler.
summary.outcome: Implemented the endpoint across 3 files, merged to PR #767, then applied two Greptile review fixes (tracing for dispatch errors and empty-body response for deduplicated commands) in a follow-up commit.

repo: All Slack routes (events, interactions, commands) live inside a single `#[cfg(feature = "slack")] { ... }` block in gateway.rs starting at line 274, closing at line 506.
Slack slash command webhook must return the response text directly in the HTTP response body (synchronous response), unlike event webhooks.
Deduplication blocks exist on all three Slack routes in gateway.rs; commands dedup should return empty body rather than the same response as events/interactions.

code.finding: Dispatch errors should be traced with tracing instrumentation (added as Greptile P1 fix).
The entire Slack routing block is feature-gated under `#[cfg(feature = "slack")]`; new Slack routes must go inside this block.
Deduplicated slash command requests should return an empty body (Greptile P2 fix); the dedup block for commands differs from events/interactions.

workflow: Greptile automated review runs on PRs and surfaces inline comments; fetching check run details reveals confidence score and comment count.
When editing a pattern that appears multiple times in a file (e.g., dedup blocks across three routes), provide sufficient surrounding context in the edit to target only the intended instance.

friction: Initial Edit attempt for the gateway.rs dedup block failed because the target string was not unique across the three Slack route handlers, requiring a re-read with more context before the edit could succeed.
